brief_summary: Decision-tree diagram. Root: 'does the board ship with VIA or VIAL flashed?'. Yes-branch leads to 'use what shipped'. No-branch leads to a depth-of-remap question with three terminal outcomes: VIA (one or two swaps), VIAL (layers, tap-dance, combos — the sweet spot), QMK (leader, OLED, host-aware behavior — learn C). Amber-bordered outcome boxes.
data_sources: keyboard-firmware-compared.mdx § 'Choosing without overthinking it' — 'If the board shipped with VIA or VIAL pre-flashed, use whatever it shipped with ... If the board is a hotswap and the builder will only ever swap one or two keys, VIA is enough ... If the builder wants real layers, tap-dance, or macros that do more than type a fixed string, VIAL is the right target ... If the builder wants behavior that no GUI exposes ... they are in QMK territory.'
